title: Arachnangraecum humbertii

The classification of Arachnangraecum humbertii is still a matter of research and some information might be subject to change. intro: Arachnangraecum humbertii, also known as the 'Spider Flower', is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Arachnangraecum humbertii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
